instruction: Filter on the high-selectivity device id first so MergeTree can use PREWHERE effectively (express that intent in SQL).
broken_query: SELECT ts, reading FROM chqr_tasks.t_telemetry_pw WHERE reading > 0.5 AND device_id = 'dev-7' LIMIT 10
gold_query: SELECT ts, reading FROM chqr_tasks.t_telemetry_pw PREWHERE device_id = 'dev-7' WHERE reading > 0.5 LIMIT 10
simulated_error: Predicate order forces a full scan before the cheap equality filter.
schema_hint: Wide MergeTree with device_id String and ts DateTime; readings are sparse.
setup_sql: CREATE DATABASE IF NOT EXISTS chqr_tasks; DROP TABLE IF EXISTS chqr_tasks.t_telemetry_pw; CREATE TABLE chqr_tasks.t_telemetry_pw (device_id String, ts DateTime, reading Float32) ENGINE = MergeTree() ORDER BY (device_id, ts); INSERT INTO chqr_tasks.t_telemetry_pw SELECT 'dev-7', toDateTime('2024-08-01 00:00:00') + number, if(number % 40 = 0, 0.9, 0.1) FROM numbers(200)
